channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Six months after ceasefire with Israel, people in Gaza say recovery hasn't even begun
item.description: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.
item.content:encoded: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.
item.content:encoded: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Israeli and Palestinian activists share a vision for peace in Gaza
item.description: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.
item.content:encoded: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
item.description: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.
item.content:encoded:
Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

(Image credit: Hussein Malla)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Pope Leo takes aim at 'handful of tyrants' spending billions on war amid tensions with Trump
item.description: Pope Leo XIV condemns "tyrants" fueling war with billions. His calls for global peace during his Africa trip come amid rising tensions with President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV condemns "tyrants" fueling war with billions. His calls for global peace during his Africa trip come amid rising tensions with President Trump.

(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
item.description: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.
item.content:encoded:
Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon, as Trump sounds optimistic on Iran talks
item.description: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, but Israel vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
The leaders of Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, but Israel vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon.

(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Indonesia's capital of the future faces doubts in the present
item.description: Indonesia is racing to build a new capital, promising a greener, futuristic city. But many citizens aren't convinced it will live up to the vision.
item.content:encoded:
Indonesia is racing to build a new capital, promising a greener, futuristic city. But many citizens aren't convinced it will live up to the vision.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: 3 things to know about naval blockades as the U.S. patrols the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.
item.content:encoded:
The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.

(Image credit: Jesse Monford)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman
